description_long: This is a short, cylindrical barrel or bucket with a robust, thick-walled structure, likely made of cast iron or a similar heavy metal. The object has a slightly flared base and a flat, circular top, which appears to be solid and possibly sealed, suggesting it is not currently open. The surface is matte to semi-glossy, with a dark, almost black coloration, and subtle textural details that indicate a rough, industrial finish. There are no visible handles or external attachments, and the top features some faint, raised markings or text, possibly for identification or branding. The overall form is utilitarian, with reinforced edges at the base and top for added durability. This object is typical of those found in urban or industrial environments, such as streets, sidewalks, or utility areas, where it might serve as a weight, cover, or containment vessel.
description: A short, heavy, cylindrical metal barrel or bucket with a flared base and flat, sealed top, finished in matte black with faint raised markings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flared base and flat top with visible raised markings.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the vertical cylindrical body and the thickness of the walls.
description_view_2: Rear view is similar to the front, showing the flared base and solid top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the uniform cylindrical shape and lack of handles.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: raised text or markings on top

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
